---
title: "Direct Rendering using ati driver"
date: 2006-12-10
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by spott on 2006-12-10
I can't seem to get direct rendering working on my machine.  I had it working, then I installed the fglrx drivers and now I can't get it to work again.

here is my xorg.conf:

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
```

-Andrew

### Post by Dave54 on 2006-12-10
I would recommend you look through here:
[https://wiki.ubuntu.com/HardwareSupportComponentsVideoCards]("https://wiki.ubuntu.com/HardwareSupportComponentsVideoCards")
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Video]("https://help.ubuntu.com/community/Video")

I'm not sure if anyone here can help you without a full description of every video-related piece of software installed on your system, and many hardware specs.

Good luck.
-Dave

---

### Post by spott on 2006-12-10
Well... I actually figured it out.

Apparently you need to completely remove the xorg-drivers-fglrx package from your system when you change back to the radeon driver.

Kind of annoying, but it works.

-Andrew

---
